description: A shaped piece of singing: melody carried by voice, the thing that is sung. The object companion of 'meliho' (to sing) — together they form the mel- family of voiced music. A song is a story that has learned to flow.
sound_symbolism: Shares its opening 'me-li' with 'meliho' (sing) — the same melodic rise, the humming nasal opening into the bright liquid 'li'. The verb carries the voice onward; 'melira' closes instead with the open 'ra': the song released, complete, ringing in the air.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'shia melira meliho' (they sing a song), 'phelora melira' (a beautiful song). Member of the mel- semantic family with 'meliho' (sing); an enumerated composition may take optional abstract classifier 'nophe'.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Communal singing costs nothing, consumes nothing, and binds a community — renewable culture in its purest form.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A song is ornament made of time — the voice's flowing line, rising and returning like a vine. 'Melira' names the shape that singing draws.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Songs are how pre-literate wisdom traveled: planting songs, mourning songs, songs that carry maps and genealogies. The song is the oldest book.

tags.creation: A made thing of voice and time
tags.ritual: Carrier of communal memory and ceremony
